topics.title: Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals
topics.description: Understand IR drill types and methodology
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand IR drill types
- Methodology
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a tabletop exercise for a ransomware scenario with injects and evaluation criteria
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the three types of IR drills?
topics.interviewAnswer: Tabletop (discussion-based, walk through scenarios), live-fire (technical team responds to simulated incident), and hybrid (tabletop with technical injects). Organizations that run regular drills contain breaches 54% faster.

quiz.question: What is a tabletop exercise?
What is a live-fire drill?
options: Technical team responds to simulated incident

quiz.question: How much faster do drill-running orgs contain breaches?
What is the drill lifecycle?
What did the hospital discover during drill?
Which best describes Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Incident Response Drill Fundamentals can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: IR Drill Scenario Design
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: IR Drill Scenario Design is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Design realistic IR drill scenarios
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a complete IR scenario with attack chain, injects, and decision branches
topics.interviewQuestion: What makes a good IR drill scenario?
topics.interviewAnswer: A good scenario uses a real threat actor (FIN7, APT29), defines a 3-5 technique attack chain using ATT&CK, creates injects simulating each step, designs decision points with branching outcomes, and includes expected outcomes for each branch.

quiz.question: What should a scenario be based on?
options: Real threat actor and attack chain

quiz.question: How many techniques in a good attack chain?
What are injects?
What are decision points?
What was the bank's drill based on?

topics.title: Day 3: Live-Fire Simulation Setup
topics.description: Set up live-fire environments for IR training
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Live-Fire Simulation Setup is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Set up live-fire environments for IR training
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Design a live-fire lab topology with attack infrastructure and safety measures
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the key components of a live-fire environment?
topics.interviewAnswer: Dedicated range (isolated network with VMs), attack infrastructure (C2 server, phishing server), victim infrastructure (workstations, servers, AD), detection infrastructure (SIEM, EDR, ticketing), and observation infrastructure (screen recording, chat, time tracker). Safety measures include network isolation and kill switch.

quiz.question: What is a dedicated range?
options: Isolated network with VMs for training

quiz.question: What does attack infrastructure include?
What does detection infrastructure include?
What is a kill switch?
options: Automated safety mechanism to stop exercise

quiz.question: What did the MSSP build for training?

topics.title: Day 4: IR Communication & Decision-Making
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: IR Communication & Decision-Making is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Master incident command
- Communication
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write communication protocols and executive briefing templates for ransomware incident
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the Incident Commander role in IR?
topics.interviewAnswer: The IC makes decisions and communicates to stakeholders (legal, PR, executives, regulators). Uses OODA Loop (Observe, Orient, Decide, Act) framework and 3-3-3 Rule (3 min to assess, 3 decisions, 3 actions). Status updates follow Situation-Impact-Actions-Next-Requests template.

quiz.question: What does the Incident Commander do?
What is the OODA Loop?
What is the 3-3-3 Rule?
What did the 911 drill reveal?
What template format should updates follow?

topics.title: Day 5: Capstone IR Drill Execution Project
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Capstone IR Drill Execution Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Execute a complete 4-hour IR drill
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Plan and run a 4-hour IR drill simulation with after-action report
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the timeline of a 4-hour IR drill?
topics.interviewAnswer: T+00:00 alert received, T+00:15 triage, T+00:30 escalate to L2, T+01:00 incident declared, T+02:00 containment, T+03:00 eradication, T+03:30 evidence collection, T+04:00 hot wash and after-action review.

quiz.question: When is incident declared in a 4-hour drill?
When does containment start?
What is a hot wash?
What was simulated in the bank's drill?
How many improvements were identified?
